name: Midwestern Chili
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Olive oil or other cooking oil
3 cans (1 pound, 4 ounces each) Red kidney beans (undrained)
1/8 teaspoon Crushed hot red chile peppers (or more to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large, heavy kettle over moderate heat. Add the onions and garlic; sauté, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes until golden.
2. Stir in the oregano and bay leaves, then add the ground beef. Break up the meat and cook, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink, about 10 minutes.
3. Mix in the chili powder, tomatoes (undrained), and kidney beans; bring to a simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour 15 minutes.
4. Stir in the remaining ingredients: additional chili powder, more kidney beans, salt, vinegar, and hot chile peppers. Simmer, stirring frequently, for an additional 15 minutes.
5. Serve hot or cool and freeze for later use.
Makes 10 to 12 Servings
